vulnerability.title: Enable Kernel Parameter to Use TCP Syncookies on Network Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies=1 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl config file wh

triage_decision.reason: Enabling TCP syncookies is a standard kernel parameter hardening measure that does not affect authentication, authorization, or critical services. It can be applied safely via sysctl and persisted in /etc/sysctl.d without risk of lockout.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sysctl_net_ipv4_tcp_syncookies (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_net_ipv4_tcp_syncookies). Could not verify.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly enables the runtime kernel parameter and persists the setting, which would resolve the vulnerability. The approach is minimal and reversible, and does not introduce new security risks. However, it does not address potential conflicting settings in other sysctl configuration files and does not reload all sysctl configurations after modifying the file.
concerns: Does not comment out or handle duplicate/conflicting entries in other sysctl config files (e.g., /etc/sysctl.conf or other files in /etc/sysctl.d).
No explicit reload of sysctl after editing the config file, although the runtime change is already applied, but the persistence may not be verified immediately.
suggested_improvements: Scan /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf, /run/sysctl.d/*.conf, and /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf for existing net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies lines and comment them out or remove them to avoid conflicts.
After appending/modifying /etc/sysctl.d/99-tcp_syncookies.conf, run `sysctl --system` (or `sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-tcp_syncookies.conf`) to reload and confirm the setting is applied.
Use `install -m 0644 -o root -g root -t /etc/sysctl.d 99-tcp_syncookies.conf` to create the file with correct permissions if it does not exist, instead of manual sed/write sequence.
